name: The White House
subject: 1.7 million and counting
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=46fb91fa3d389664050fa142a2fd6f5cb176b7ad39896950b99fb4252e65f9b87d82dc2d0a29356e552a622489d4fc054718f3ff543dfe61

Not a bad year

Under President Donald J. Trump, nearly 1.7 million new jobs have been created.

159,000 jobs have been added in the manufacturing industry.

Another 57,000 have been created in mining and logging.

The unemployment rate is its lowest in nearly 17 years.

And GDP growth topped 3 percent the past two quarters—after zero such quarters in 2016.

The bottom line is that while the media largely ignores it, America’s economy is firing on all cylinders. More growth means more of our citizens are able to afford their own homes, send their kids to college, and save for their retirements.

Under President Trump, Americans are getting back to work.
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=46fb91fa3d389664b39d3b822dfddea301b98a24279ee89e8e6fa4f5fc390aa815516408c766c9f8849e8e2ec1244c290c7758da5ec4e830
Take a look for yourself.

What tax reform is all about

Tax cuts are an early centerpiece of what President Trump promised to do once elected: Run a government that’s more accountable, more democratic, and less eager to line its pockets with tax money from working families and small businesses.

In addition to tax relief for individuals, this bill is about restoring America’s economic dominance by putting U.S. companies on a level playing field with foreign competitors.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=46fb91fa3d3896644645f30b7e696974c55a3e13868f025a0ae91d70e77e1ced3c56c46b06323442fbc2214e66c6e07d5bffed6575cb1bd2
Read how the Tax Cuts Act will put America first .

Bonus: A 30-second cut of
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=46fb91fa3d389664b94ef529e8bfb511e33a93048dbdacb598d0a224c5a735acdfcb02ad43a43025c75863fbf5128470f721847d96cec3eb
President Trump and Congressional leaders on the White House South Lawn.

A surprise for Christmas

Ahead of next week’s holiday, Vice President Mike Pence made a surprise visit to U.S. troops stationed in Afghanistan to thank them for their service. The Vice President also met with Afghan President Ashraf Ghani to discuss the country’s ongoing security partnership with the United States.

President Trump met with service members yesterday, as well, making a visit to Walter Reed’s USO Warrior & Family Center to visit military personnel recovering from injuries.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=46fb91fa3d389664d14e265f565d8d5cd961537efa919a07438b7dee7dbc5ebd88997ef6f6c0f40b3f3b397b67a92878cbef3bbf46eeff93
See what Vice President Pence told U.S. troops in Afghanistan.

Photo of the Day

Vice President Mike Pence delivers remarks to American troops in Afghanistan | December 21, 2017 (Official White House Photo by D. Myles Cullen)

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=46fb91fa3d389664dcc119a6d61dd365b3cbc71a2e9287fc4aeca56f262908f3ad28275b40337113332a0c19d83055c70946c6487913d92e

Contact the White House |

name: Russ Feingold, LegitAction
subject: Big strides, as early as next year
body:
[ Legit ]LegitAction

Friend,

LegitAction members have an opportunity next year to bring
groundbreaking progress to our democracy -- including transforming our
presidential elections.

We’re going to keep pushing for Connecticut, Oregon, and other states
to join the National Popular Vote Interstate Compact so votes in every
state matter. We’re going to push for automatic voter registration in
Massachusetts and around the country so more people cast ballots. And
we’re going to keep pushing to restore the ability to vote to folks
with convictions in Florida and California.

These are things we can and will get done to meaningfully improve
legitimacy, even with President Trump in the White House and his
enablers controlling Congress.

But in order to make our next election more legitimate than our last
one, we have to start 2018 with the resources to push hard. So I’m
asking you and your fellow supporters to contribute an additional
$15,000 before 2017 ends on December 31.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/go/3463?t=1002&akid=482%2E356062%2EDxv8Oy ]Help LegitAction raise an additional $15,000 before December ends
so we can start 2018 pushing hard for more legitimate elections. Make
a contribution of $3 now.

In the battle for our democratic legitimacy, LegitAction members are
committed to holding our ground against right-wing threats while
pushing to advance progressive reforms.

We will keep making it clear just how illegitimate the sham
Trump-Pence election commission is. We will keep fighting against
hyperpartisan gerrymandering. But we can also help pass automatic
voter registration bills, re-enfranchise felons, and move closer to a
true national popular vote.

And if we do all that, we’ll end up with an election that’s far more
legitimate than we could have imagined just a year ago.

Our plans for next year are big, but our capacity for making a
difference is even bigger. Let’s hit the ground running in January by
meeting our goal of raising $15,000 more before the year ends.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/go/3463?t=1003&akid=482%2E356062%2EDxv8Oy ]Help LegitAction hit the ground running in 2018. Make a
contribution of $3 now to
help reach our goal of raising another $15,000 in grassroots
contributions before 2017 ends.

Legit members have the ability to make big strides for legitimacy in
our presidential elections as early as next year. But we have to
prepare now.

Thank you for standing up for democratic legitimacy today,

Russ Feingold
LegitAction

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: The GOP delivered a giant, gift-wrapped present to the big banks
disclaimer_text: paid for by millions of working families
body:
In a vote on the Senate floor in the dead of night, the Republicans delivered a giant, gift-wrapped present to big banks like Wells Fargo, giant corporations, and billionaires, paid for by millions of working families.
---
[](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dscc-ewarren?refcode=DPE-20171222-Warren-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
Team,

Corrupt. That's a pretty tough word. Tough, but how else does anyone explain what the Republicans just did.

In a vote on the Senate floor in the dead of night, the Republicans delivered a giant, gift-wrapped present to big banks like Wells Fargo, giant corporations, and billionaires. And who will pay for this present? Millions of working families.

Most Americans see this bill for what it is: A giveaway to the rich and powerful. But Republicans in Congress didn't care what working Americans thought. They proved that they're not here to work for the people. They're here to work for the donors who fund their campaigns and Super PACs.

It's bad enough that the Republicans are handing over billions in giveaways to the rich, but the American people will be paying for generations to come.

We've been fighting our hearts out all year to resist and persist -- and our work has been powerfully important. But here's the harsh reality: When Republicans control the House, the Senate, and the White House, billionaire megadonors who fund the Paul Ryans and Mitch McConnells of the world will call the shots. That means a tax bill that is a payoff to those donors can breeze through Congress and rig the system even more for the rich and powerful.

So what's next? The answer: We fight back. We fight back and we take back control. We're eleven months away from Election Day -- our biggest opportunity to stop the GOP agenda dead in its tracks. I'm in this fight all the way, but I can't do it alone.

[**Help us send a message to the GOP: We 're going to remind America that Republicans sold out the American people -- remind them today, tomorrow and every day until Election Day. Chip in $1 to elect Senate Democrats and replace the GOP in 2018.**](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dscc-ewarren?refcode=DPE-20171222-Warren-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")

We're angry. We're frustrated. We're disgusted. But we will NOT lose hope. The best way to stop Donald Trump, Mitch McConnell and Paul Ryan and turn this country around is to fight back. And that is exactly what we will do.

We might not have billionaire megadonors in our back pocket -- but we don't need them. We have you -- and if we stand together, today and every day, we will fight, and we will win.

**Don 't stop fighting now. Chip in $1 to elect Senate Democrats, stop the Republican agenda in 2018 and help level the playing field for working families:**

[**http://www.dscc.org/fight-back-now**](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dscc-ewarren?refcode=DPE-20171222-Warren-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")

Thanks for being a part of this,

Elizabeth

name: Larry Cohen, Our Revolution
subject: Unity Reform Commission Results - Help Transform the Democratic Party
body:
#
Friends,

At Our Revolution, we work for change inside and outside the Democratic Party. We work on local, state and national issues, support candidates that support our issues, and organize politically. That's why we were excited when Sen. Bernie Sanders pushed for the creation of the Unity and Reform Commission during the 2016 election and appointed several allies and Our Revolution board members to the Commission.
Since the creation of the Commission, I've worked with my fellow appointees - Our Revolution president Nina Turner and board members Lucy Flores, Jane Kleeb and Jim Zogby, and Gus Newport, Nomiki Konst and Jeff Weaver -- to push the Democratic National Committee (DNC) to be a model for that which it seeks. We did this by advocating for policies that would expand the base of the Democratic Party. Through four working groups (Caucuses, Primaries, Unpledged Delegates and Party Reform), we offered recommendations such as:
* Converting 60 percent of the Superdelegates to pledged delegates based on
the results of presidential primaries and caucuses.
* Electoral reforms including same-day registration, same day party change, and absentee voting in caucus states
* Greater disclosure of Democratic National Committee spending and an Ombudsman Council of elected DNC members to review complaints about Charter and Bylaws violations
* An open nominating process for party leadership positions at the DNC and in all state parties, based on greater diversity and differences in viewpoints.

Now, the recommendations will go to the 447-person DNC and we need your help! Will you take a moment to read the Commission report, [found here](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7adf5/bed43eb/3c3c9a44/75fe8bd1/1066397483/VEsH/ "https://dnc.app.box.com/s/55lk3avwwnpqu2g00sdyd1i6ylz9fz0b"), and commit to working for reform in your state and urging the DNC to adopt the Commission's full report?
If you are committed to transforming the Democratic Party from the inside, please work with us to push the DNC to expeditiously adopt reforms outlined by the Unity and Reform Commission. As you know, voting is the cornerstone of our democracy. It's not enough to criticize Republicans for attacking the right to vote; Democrats must examine their own internal policies that restrict voting as well.
****[Sign up for next steps](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7adf5/bed43eb/3c3c9a44/75fe8bd0/1066397483/VEsE/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/join-the-urc-fight")****
Solidarity,

Larry Cohen
Our Revolution Board Chair
PAID FOR BY OUR REVOLUTION
603 2ND ST, NE
WASHINGTON, DC, 20002

Not authorized by any candidate or candidate committee. Our Revolution is a 501(c)(4) organization. Donations to Our Revolution are not deductible as charitable contributions for Federal income tax purposes. All donations are made to support Our Revolution's general mission and are not designated for any specific activity.
603 2nd St NE, Washington, DC 20002

clean_body: Friends, At Our Revolution, we work for change inside and outside the Democratic Party. We work on local, state and national issues, support candidates that support our issues, and organize politically. That's why we were excited when Sen. Bernie Sanders pushed for the creation of the Unity and Reform Commission during the 2016 election and appointed several allies and Our Revolution board members to the Commission. Since the creation of the Commission, I've worked with my fellow appointees - Our Revolution president Nina Turner and board members Lucy Flores, Jane Kleeb and Jim Zogby, and Gus Newport, Nomiki Konst and Jeff Weaver -- to push the Democratic National Committee (DNC) to be a model for that which it seeks. We did this by advocating for policies that would expand the base of the Democratic Party. Through four working groups (Caucuses, Primaries, Unpledged Delegates and Party Reform), we offered recommendations such as: Converting 60 percent of the Superdelegates to pledged delegates based on the results of presidential primaries and caucuses. Electoral reforms including same-day registration, same day party change, and absentee voting in caucus states Greater disclosure of Democratic National Committee spending and an Ombudsman Council of elected DNC members to review complaints about Charter and Bylaws violations An open nominating process for party leadership positions at the DNC and in all state parties, based on greater diversity and differences in viewpoints. Now, the recommendations will go to the 447-person DNC and we need your help! Will you take a moment to read the Commission report, "", and commit to working for reform in your state and urging the DNC to adopt the Commission's full report? If you are committed to transforming the Democratic Party from the inside, please work with us to push the DNC to expeditiously adopt reforms outlined by the Unity and Reform Commission. As you know, voting is the cornerstone of our democracy. It's not enough to criticize Republicans for attacking the right to vote; Democrats must examine their own internal policies that restrict voting as well. ** ""** Solidarity, Larry Cohen Our Revolution Board Chair PAID FOR BY OUR REVOLUTION 603 2ND ST, NE WASHINGTON, DC, 20002 Not authorized by any candidate or candidate committee. subject: Best Christmas Gift Ever
body:
| | |

| **_Best Christmas Gift Ever_** As Christmas quickly approaches, **President Donald Trump** , **Vice President Mike Pence** and** ** Indiana's entire Republican Congressional delegation**** gave Hoosiers a big Christmas gift. Starting next year, tax reform will deliver larger paychecks for Hoosiers across the state.

Working overtime this holiday season, our Republican leaders in Washington -- **Senator Todd Young, Rep. Jackie Walorski, Rep. Jim Banks, Rep. Todd Rokita, Rep. Susan Brooks, Rep. Luke Messer, Rep. Larry Bucshon** and**Rep. Trey Hollingsworth **\-- delivered on a big promise to simplify our tax code, lower rates across the board and add more jobs to our growing economy.

And like he usually does, Democrat Joe Donnelly sided with his Washington party bosses -- and against working Hoosiers -- again on this historic, job-creating reform.

At its core, tax reform lowers tax rates at every income level. The standard deduction is doubled, and the child tax credit is doubled to $2,000. Many popular tax credits, deductions and tax-advantaged programs are retained -- including the adoption tax credit, mortgage deductions, education savings programs and retirement accounts.

And, to help cap off this historic legislation, tax reform also repeals the Obamacare individual mandate and cuts taxes for job creators, allowing them to add jobs, raise wages and grow our economy.

As we're unwrapping the gifts under the tree this year at the Hupfer house, I'll take a second to thank our elected leaders in Washington for allowing Hooseirs to keep more of what they earn.

>From my family to yours, we wish you a very Merry Christmas.

-Kyle Hupfer

_Photo: Chairman Kyle Hupfer, his wife, Jill, and their children Ashton, Adah and Archer take a special family picture for Christmas near their home in Pendleton, Indiana. _

| What a year! **Governor Eric Holcomb** has traversed the state and the world, listening to Hoosiers and telling Indiana's success story far and wide. From his inauguration in January to these final weeks of the year -- where he's [breaking fundraising records](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=50f40be808&e=1acd9bb3d6) \-- Governor Holcomb is working nonstop to elevate Indiana and improve the lives of all Hoosiers.

This week, Governor Holcomb began wrapping up his first year in office by hosting the 22nd Annual Governor’s Luncheon for Scouting in Indianapolis for the state's Boy Scouts!

At the luncheon, he highlighted the importance of the Boy Scout Law and civility in today's society:

**"C _ivility doesn’t mean we put all our differences aside. But it demands that we listen to & respect people, their ideas & different points of view other than our own. There’s a definition I like – even though it doesn’t explicitly use the word 'civility.' It’s the Boy Scout Law. A Scout is trustworthy, loyal, helpful, friendly, courteous, kind, obedient, cheerful, thrifty, brave, clean & reverent.

"I’m going to demand civility of those I work with, of Hoosier legislators of both parties, and of myself. When you come across people with ideas different than your own, don’t dismiss them. Hear them out. Be civil. You will be the stronger for it... and so will we."_**

We hope you can **join us for the full conference** ([agenda here](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=13382eda88&e=1acd9bb3d6)) -- which will teach you the skills you need to help achieve more Republican victories in 2018 -- but if you can only join us for part of the conference, this week we opened up [special registration options](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=256974653b&e=1acd9bb3d6):

Governor Suzanne Crouch** ($25/person).

**3\. Attend[just the Saturday U.S. Senate Straw Poll](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=f338e42f84&e=1acd9bb3d6)**. **_A Note From Jeanie_**
**Dear Derek,**

body:
**NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester Statement on Representative Kathy Rogers Pleading Guilty**
**For Immediate Release:** 12/22/2017
**Contact:** Patrick Hynes, (202) 321-5310
CONCORD - NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester released the following statement on Democrat Representative Kathy Rogers pleading guilty to assaulting an election recount volunteer:
"The act of assaulting someone is unacceptable for anyone to commit – and when it is politically motivated by an elected official, it is especially despicable. Today, Democrat Representative Kathy Rogers pleaded guilty to assaulting a volunteer during a 2016 election recount. This news is startling and should lead directly to Representative Rogers' resignation from office. The fact that Democrat Party Chair Raymond Buckley and Democrat Minority Leader Steve Shurtleff showed up to signal their support for her is deeply disappointing. We encourage New Hampshire Democrat leaders to send a strong message to Granite Staters that violence against anyone is not acceptable by demanding Representative Rogers to resign effective immediately."
###
